description: A path that reaches its end soon is 'teku'. A brief wait takes the same adjective, so it covers limited length and duration. 'mulu' (low) concerns height or vertical position; 'teku' answers 'laeno' (long) on its own scale.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into true-mid front 'e'. The back of the tongue then closes against the soft palate for 'k' and opens into rounded high-back 'u'. Stress falls on 'te'.
sound_symbolism: Dental 't' and velar 'k' place a firm closure at the start of each syllable. The compact two-beat form finishes briskly, a useful match for short extent.
usage_notes: 'teku ruela' is a short path and 'teku shemu' a short moment. The adjective can describe something complete whose extent happens to be limited.

semantic_domains.spatial: limited end-to-end extent in a path or object
